---
title: "Wafer maps"
description: "Tile plots of die-level KPIs using the bundled example dataset."
---

## Setup

```{python}
#| label: setup
import plotnine as pn
pn.theme_set(pn.theme_minimal())

from slimnine.example_data import load_wafer_dataset
from slimnine.wafer_maps import (
    WaferMapConfig,
    plot_wafermap_diverge,
    plot_wafermap_passfail,
    plot_wafermap_spectral,
)

df = load_wafer_dataset()
df.shape
kpis = ['vt', 'idsat', 'ileak', 'freq']
```


## Data


```{python}
df.head()
```

The bundled dataset has 3 lots × 6 wafers × ~9k die. KPIs are `vt`, `idsat`,
`ileak`, `freq`; the boolean `pass` column is derived from spec limits on
the other three.


## KPI spectral

A `lot_id × wafer_no` grid surfaces both lot-level shifts (rows) and
within-lot variation (columns).

```{python}
#| label: ileak-grid-all
cfg = WaferMapConfig(
    facet=pn.facet_wrap("wafer_id", nrow = 3),
    fig_size=(14, 8),
    panel_spacing=0.01
)

for kpi in kpis:
    plot = plot_wafermap_spectral(df, kpi=kpi, cfg=cfg)
    plot.show()
```

## Pass / fail

```{python}
#| label: pass-single
plot_wafermap_passfail(df, kpi="pass", cfg=cfg)
```
